name: Gnocchi Di Semolina
recipeInstructions:
These gnocchi are sometimes thought to be a Roman specialty, but they are actually eaten throughout Italy.
Heat the milk with a pinch of salt until boiling. Gradually add the semolina while continuously stirring with a wooden spoon to prevent lumps from forming. Continue cooking and stirring for 20 minutes until thickened. Remove from heat and stir in 2 tablespoons of melted butter. Then, gradually incorporate 2 tablespoons of Parmesan cheese, followed by one egg yolk at a time, a pinch of pepper, and nutmeg.
Spread the semolina mixture onto a large baking dish or clean marble slab to about 1/2-inch thickness using a cold wet spatula. Allow it to cool slightly.
Preheat the oven to 350°F (175°C). Melt the remaining butter and lightly grease the baking dish. Cut squares or circles from the dough and place them in the greased dish, drizzling with butter and sprinkling with Parmesan cheese. Add a second layer of gnocchi and repeat as necessary. Finally, sprinkle breadcrumbs over the top before baking for about 20 minutes or until golden brown.
